description: Broadened the workload to require helper-argument handoff, then added taskExpandArgs to both warcraft_task_create and warcraft_status so agents can invoke warcraft_task_expand directly without reconstructing which pending manual tasks or mode should be promoted. This makes the higher-level promotion path as easy to call as the lower-level planWriteArgs path.

Broadened the workload to require follow-up arg handoff, then added ready-to-use planApproveArgs/taskSyncArgs to scaffold-promotion responses from warcraft_plan_write(useScaffold) and warcraft_task_expand. This lets agents carry the reviewed promotion flow forward without reconstructing the approval/sync calls after the scaffold has been written.

Broadened the workload to require status-level follow-up arg handoff, then taught warcraft_status to return planApproveArgs for draft plans and taskSyncArgs for approved-but-unsynced plans. This lets agents recover the next exact approval/sync calls from status after promotion work has started, complementing the existing scaffold, planWriteArgs, and taskExpandArgs handoffs.

Broadened the workload to require ordered promotion-flow handoff, then added structured promotionFlow sequences across task/status/plan responses. Escalated manual work now returns the reviewed chain in order (task_expand → review → plan_approve → tasks_sync), draft plans return the remaining review/approve/sync flow, and plan approval returns the exact sync follow-up. This reduces orchestration friction without hiding the explicit review gate.

Broadened the workload to cover draft plans that only partially absorb manual work, then taught warcraft_task_expand, warcraft_plan_write, and warcraft_status to keep surfacing remainingManualTasks plus follow-up taskExpandArgs/promotionFlow until all pending manual tasks are merged into the reviewed draft. This closes a real gap in the new merge-into-draft path by keeping agents from approving too early when manual work still sits outside the plan.

Broadened the workload to require withholding premature approval handoff, then stopped advertising planApproveArgs/taskSyncArgs while draft plans still leave manual tasks outside the reviewed plan. Partial expansion/write flows now surface remainingManualTasks plus follow-up taskExpandArgs/promotionFlow, but they no longer suggest approval/sync until the draft actually covers the pending manual work.

Broadened the workload to require a real approval gate for incomplete drafts, then taught warcraft_plan_approve to reject plans that still leave unmatched manual tasks outside the reviewed draft. Combined with the earlier remainingManualTasks/taskExpandArgs handoff, this closes the loop: incomplete drafts no longer advertise approval, and a direct approval attempt now fails with specific guidance to re-run warcraft_task_expand first.

Broadened the workload to require actionable blocked-approval recovery hints, then enriched the incomplete-draft approval failure path with explicit hints: an exact warcraft_task_expand recovery command and a retry warcraft_plan_approve reminder. This makes the new approval gate easier to recover from without weakening it.

Broadened the workload to require structured blocked-approval recovery metadata, then extended toolError so failures can carry machine-readable data/warnings. warcraft_plan_approve now returns blockedReason, remainingManualTasks, taskExpandArgs, retryArgs, and warning metadata when approval is blocked by unmatched manual tasks outside the draft plan. This makes recovery from blocked promotion steps as structured as the success-path handoffs.
